turns.thought: [OBSERVE] The swarm has completely converged: diversity is essentially zero (normalized ~2e-14), velocities are zero for all particles (velocity_zero_ratio=1.0), and all fitness values are nearly identical (std ~2e-16). No improvement in gbest for 200 iterations. The current best fitness 0.098 is not the global minimum (0), indicating the swarm is trapped in a suboptimal local minimum. The boundary hit ratio is 0, so particles are not at edges but clustered tightly in the interior. | [DIAGNOSE] This is classic premature convergence to a deep local minimum. The swarm has collapsed onto a single point where the gradient is zero, and all particles have lost momentum. The root cause is the multimodal Griewank landscape: the swarm has discovered one of many local basins and cannot escape because there is no diversity to generate explorative steps. Even a minor perturbation would likely return to the same basin because all particles are at the same location. A large-scale reinitialization of a subset of particles is required to explore other regions. | [STRATEGIZE] The goal is to break the convergence and reintroduce diversity to search for the global optimum. Resetting a significant fraction of the worst particles (which are all equally bad) to random positions across the search space will inject fresh exploration, while preserving the best-so-far information in the remaining particles. Using a high inertia weight after reset will encourage continued exploration. This is better than parameter-only adjustments (which can't fix zero-velocity collapse) or opposition reinit (since particles are not at boundaries). Levy flight might also work, but a random reset is simpler and will fully explore the space.

turns.thought: [REFLECT] The reset of 15 particles did not immediately improve the global best, which is expected because the new random positions are unlikely to hit a better basin instantly. The intervention should have restored diversity (new random positions and velocities), allowing the swarm to explore other regions in subsequent iterations. The unchanged best confirms that the original converged point remains the best found so far, but the swarm now has the potential to escape the local minimum given time. | [REVISED_DIAGNOSE] Premature convergence to a deep local minimum was correctly identified. The reset was an appropriate response and has likely reintroduced diversity. The lack of immediate improvement does not contradict the diagnosis; the swarm requires several iterations to exploit the new diversity.
